uplink.description: measurement frame: liquid detected (digital input 0 high) with temperature
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds frame; byte0=0xA0 selects a non-timestamped measurement frame, block 0x01 carries the digital-input states (bit0=input0=liquid probe, high=leak), block 0x08 carries signed temperature in hundredths of a degree (0x0A28=2600 -> 26.00 C). Expected produced by running reference/upstream-codec.js (entree.value[0][0]=1, temperature0.value[0]=26).

uplink.description: measurement frame: no liquid (digital input 0 low)
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds frame; same measurement layout with the digital-input byte 0x00 (no input active). Expected produced by running reference/upstream-codec.js (entree.value[0][0]=0).

Synthetic in-bounds frame; byte0=0x81 selects a non-timestamped life frame, then tensionv=0x0C80=3200 mV (-> 3.2 V) and tensionc=0x0C00. Expected produced by running reference/upstream-codec.js (tensionv.value=3.2).

uplink.description: device error frame yields an error
uplink._note: Synthetic frame; byte0=0x8E selects the ATIM error frame (low nibble 0xE). Upstream reference/upstream-codec.js classifies this as 'Trame d'erreur'; we surface it as a decode error since it carries no measurement.
